stimulus: The Great Smog of London was a period of severe air pollution that engulfed the city in 1952. During those few days, thick fog and coal smoke ______blank visibility across nearly every neighborhood. They contributed to thousands of deaths from respiratory illness.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is verb tense. The preceding sentence establishes that the events took place in 1952, a specific time in the past, so the simple past tense "reduced" is required.

Choice A is incorrect because the present progressive "are reducing" doesn't match the past time frame. Choice C is incorrect because the future perfect "will have reduced" doesn't match the past time frame. Choice D is incorrect because the simple future "will reduce" doesn't match the past time frame.
